{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014p3bnPwqfG8Pz7iBkvhuw3","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: # Listener implementation\\n2: \\n3: ## Socket handling\\n4: \\n5: Envoy uses the following procedure for creating sockets and assigning them to workers.\\n6: \\n7: 1. When a listener is created, a socket is pre-created for every worker on the main thread. This\\n8:    allows most errors to be caught early on in the listener creation process (e.g., bad socket\\n9:    option, unable to bind, etc.).\\n10:     * If using reuse_port, a unique socket is created for every worker.\\n11:     * If not using reuse_port, a unique socket is created for worker 0, and then that socket\\n12:       is duplicated for all other workers.\\n13: 2. In the case of a listener update in which the address/socket is to be shared (i.e., a named\\n14:    listener that has configuration settings changed but listens on the same address will share\\n15:    sockets with the draining listener), all sockets are duplicated regardless of whether using\\n16:    reuse_port or not. This keeps the logic equivalent, and also makes sure that when using\\n17:    reuse_port no TCP connections are dropped during the drain process (in the race condition between\\n18:    assigning a queue and closing the listening socket on the old listener).\\n19: 3. In the case of hot restart, all sockets are requested by worker index. If the old process has\\n20:    a match, the socket is duplicated and sent to the new process. Thus, hot restart and a listener\\n21:    update effectively share the same update process. This is so that no connections are dropped\\n22:    when using reuse_port.\\n23: 4. A consequence of every listener having dedicated sockets (whether duplicated or not) is that\\n24:    a listener can close() its sockets when removed without concern for other listeners.\\n25: \\n26: For more information, see `ListenSocketFactoryImpl` and its use within `ListenerImpl` and\\n27: `ListenerManagerImpl`.\\n28: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.33.0@75cca99ee9fea5a6b699af6a2124ec7404fe5d13/-/blob/source/docs/listener.md\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01RUcQhhvBTTzmRMhC7N8xCo","session_id":"2f458330-a4f4-41c8-ba90-0d93f669b837","uuid":"18ced452-c291-49c4-9017-a929a9676afa"}

}\n710: \n711: std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n712: ListenerManagerImpl::listeners(ListenerState state) {\n713: ASSERT(!workers_started_);\n946:   workers_started_ = true;\n947:   uint32_t i = 0;\n948: \n949:   absl::BlockingCounter workers_waiting_to_run(workers_.size());\n950:   std::function<void()> it results in a issue if Envoy is\n955:   // killed before workers are actually started. Specifically the AdminRequestGetStatsAndKill test\n956:   // case in main_common_test fails with ASAN error if we use \"Cleanup\" here.\n957:   const auto listeners_pending_init =\n958:       std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size() * active_listeners_.size());\n959:   ASSERT(warming_listeners_.empty());\n960:   // We need to protect against inline deletion so have to use           draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\\n865:         for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\\n866:           // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we }\\n935:   }\\n936: \\n937:   stats_.listener_removed_.inc();\\n938:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\\n939:   return true;\\n940: }\\n941: \\n942: absl::Status **existing_active_listener));\\n607:     // In this case we have no warming listener, so what we do depends on whether workers\\n608:     // have been started or not.\\n609:     if (workers_started_) What we do depends on\\n618:     // whether workers have been started or not.\\n619:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(name, *new_listener));\\n620:     if (workers_started_) {\\n621:       new_listener->debugLog(\\\"add warming listener\\\");\\n622:       warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\\n623:     } else {\\n624:       new_listener->debugLog(\\\"add active listener\\\");\\n625:       active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\\n626:     }\\n627: \\n628:     added = true;\\n629:   }\\n630: \\n631:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\\n632:   if (added) {\\n633:     stats_.listener_added_.inc();\\n634:   } else {\\n635:     stats_.listener_modified_.inc();\\n636:   }\\n637: \\n638:   new_listener_ref.initialize();\\n639:   return true;\\n640: }\\n641: \\n642: bool ListenerManagerImpl::hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\\n643:                                                            const ListenerImpl& listener) {\\n644:   for (const auto& existing_listener : list) {\\n645:     if (existing_listener->hasDuplicatedAddress(listener)) {\\n646:       return true;\\n647:     }\\n648:   }\\n649:   return false;\\n650: }\\n651: \\n652: void ListenerManagerImpl::drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener) {\\n653:   // First add the listener to the draining list.\\n654:   std::list<DrainingListener>::iterator draining_it = draining_listeners_.emplace(\\n655:       draining_listeners_.begin(), std::move(listener), workers_.size());\\n656: \\n657:   // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\\n658:   // restart.
